data.short_description: Couch Potato, a restaurant specializing in baked potatoes, walking tacos, hot dogs, and spudwiches, has opened at 1 Eden Avenue in Southington in a historic 1941 building previously home to Southington News and Dominic's Men's Shop. The eatery previously operated in Waterbury and Wallingford. Hours are Monday–Saturday 10:30 AM–9:00 PM, closed Sunday.

data.store_name: Lu-Mac's Package Store / Jimmies Ice Cream Shoppe
data.short_description: Lu-Mac's Package Store and Jimmies Ice Cream Shoppe are relocating to a new farmhouse/country barn-style building adjacent to their current location at 356 Route 2 in Preston. The new building will feature a full-sized NASCAR race car suspended from the ceiling. Construction is actively underway as of April 2026.

data.location: 122 Prospect Hill Road, Sophia's Plaza, East Windsor, CT
data.short_description: Molly's Sweet Spot, a bakery owned by local resident Molly Francia, is opening in June 2026 at Sophia's Plaza in East Windsor. The shop will offer custom cakes, cupcakes, gourmet cookies, cakesicles, brownies, dirty sodas, and more with both walk-in and custom order options.

data.location: 501 Evergreen Way, The Shops at Evergreen Walk, South Windsor, CT
data.short_description: CollectUConn featuring The Gampel Collection is opening at The Shops at Evergreen Walk in South Windsor, adjacent to Disibio Shoes and across from Old Navy. The store will sell game-used lockers, limited-edition seats, championship banner tote bags, and commemorative pieces from Gampel Pavilion's renovation. A portion of proceeds benefits UConn Athletics; online shopping is also available at collectuconn.com.

data.location: 1447 Whalley Avenue, Whalley Commons, New Haven, CT
data.short_description: 7 Brew Coffee is opening its New Haven location on April 13, 2026 at Whalley Commons on Whalley Avenue. Pre-opening events include a charity weekend (April 3–5), a friends and family free drink weekend (April 10–12), and community free drink hours during opening week. The drive-through chain serves flavored coffees, energy drinks, and smoothies.

Sprouts Farmers Market is planning a 26,000+ square foot store at 1052 Boston Post Road in Milford on the site of the former Hooters restaurant and an adjacent Genesis dealership property. This would be the first CT Sprouts location, with another planned for Westport. The project is currently in the site plan phase with no opening date announced.

Ross Dress for Less is eyeing a ~30,000 square foot former Big Lots space at 1150 Union Street in West Springfield, MA, with a tentative opening in Fall 2026. The opening is contingent on lease finalization and construction permits. Ross has also signed a lease for a Chicopee location as part of its broader East Coast expansion.

Toor Wine & Liquor has opened at 212 Talcottville Road in Vernon, part of a brand new plaza. The store offers a large selection of wine, liquors, beer, and single cans. Hours: Monday–Saturday 9am–9pm, Sunday 10am–6pm.

Shogun X Sushi has opened at 1483 Pleasant Valley Road in Manchester, across from The Plaza at Buckland Hills, in the former Hooters space. All-you-can-eat Japanese restaurant featuring a conveyor belt and train food delivery system, IPad ordering, and claw machines. Adult Lunch $27.99, Adult Dinner $37.99. Hours: Mon–Thu 11am–10pm, Fri–Sun 11am–10:30pm.

Mozz & Co, an upscale-casual Italian deli, is opening April 8, 2026 at 22 Raymond Road in West Hartford. Owned by chef Joey Cusano (former executive chef at Le Mazet). Serves chicken cutlets, porchetta, broccoli rabe, house-made meats, salads, and Italian desserts. Hours: Mon–Sat 11am–5pm, Sun 11am–3pm.

data.location: The Shops at Evergreen Walk, South Windsor, CT (former Lux Bond & Green space)
data.short_description: Pandora jewelry store is under construction at The Shops at Evergreen Walk in South Windsor, in the former Lux Bond & Green space next to Brighton Collectibles. Construction permit valued at ~$100,000. Opening date TBD.

Mansfield Drive-In has opened for its 72nd consecutive season. Double features shown nightly. Adult tickets: $14.50; Children/Military/Seniors: $10.50. Flea market Sundays 8 AM–2 PM, $3/vehicle. Online ticket pre-purchase available.

data.location: Main Street & Center Street, Manchester, CT
data.short_description: Manchester is eyeing a roundabout at Main Street and Center Street to improve safety, reduce congestion, and create a welcoming downtown gateway. Conceptual rendering shared via community Facebook groups. Town is applying for state LOTCIP funding and seeking community feedback.

data.location: Cedar Corner, 930 High Ridge Road, Stamford, CT
data.short_description: PopUp Bagels is opening its 5th Connecticut location at Cedar Corner in Stamford, between Open Rice and NP Jewelers. Offers fresh daily bagels (plain, everything, poppyseed, salt, sesame), schmears, butters, salmon, coffee, and juices. Chain has 25+ national locations. No opening date announced.

Crystal Mall in Waterford is undergoing major restructuring. Temporary construction walls limit access to 5 remaining active retailers: Men's Wearhouse, Sakura Japan, H&R Block, Lenscrafters, and Buffalo Wild Wings. Electric Boat is constructing a new facility inside for office space, research capabilities, and training operations, replacing the traditional open concourse.

data.location: The Shops at Farmington Valley, 110 Albany Turnpike, Canton, CT
data.short_description: CAVA Mediterranean fast-casual restaurant is opening at The Shops at Farmington Valley in Canton, taking over part of the former Talbots space. Serves customizable bowls and wraps with falafel, shawarma, lamb, grilled chicken, and steak. CAVA's 8th CT location (others in Newington, Westport, Greenwich, Danbury, Shelton, plus Glastonbury under construction). Opening date TBD.

data.location: Former Big Lots location, Bristol, CT
data.short_description: American Liquidations, a high-quality discount retailer (not a bin store), is coming to the former Big Lots location in Bristol, sharing the ~45,000 sq ft building with Aldi (~25,000 sq ft each). Will carry furniture, tools, appliances, hardware, and merchandise pallets (new/overstock/open-box/returns). Currently operates in Waterbury and Milford.

Olde World Apizza in North Haven has been sold to three seasoned restaurateurs: Robert Marcarelli (30 years culinary experience, Pi Restaurant Consulting), Jared Schulefand (owner of Home restaurant, 14 years), and Israel Lopez (Grand Apizza North Haven background). The 30-year legacy, recipes, traditions, and existing staff are being maintained.
